program.name: GLASS
program.fullName: Global Leaders and Scholars in STEM
program.mission: A true global leader is a visionary who can explore the intricacies of our undeniably interconnected world with elegance and insight.
program.description: GLASS is a three-year honors initiative at NYU Tandon that combines research, global experiences, and interdisciplinary learning. Scholars connect their engineering work to the NAE Grand Challenges and UN Sustainable Development Goals — building the skills to solve the world's most pressing problems.
program.abstract:
In my paper, I look into how artificial intelligence can be engineered to advance global educational equity, a focus that was shaped by my experience in the GLASS program. I have seen how AI-driven systems transform industries through personalization, automation, and scalability, yet their benefits remain unevenly distributed especially in education. Despite the rapid growth of digital learning tools, millions of students worldwide still lack access to reliable internet, devices, and quality instruction. Through GLASS, I came to understand that this gap is not simply a technological limitation, but a systems-level challenge shaped by infrastructure, design, and access.

Drawing from research on adaptive learning and intelligent tutoring systems, I explore how AI can improve student outcomes through real-time feedback, personalized pacing, and data-driven instruction. At the same time, I address critical risks such as algorithmic bias and inequity in data representation that can further marginalize the students we aim to serve inclusively. My academic and professional experiences in software engineering and machine learning have reinforced this perspective, showing me how technologies are often optimized for efficiency and performance rather than accessibility or equity.

I focus on AI-powered tutoring platforms as scalable solutions, particularly when designed for low-resource environments. Inspired by both my global exposure through GLASS and my hands-on technical experience, I propose the development of lightweight, offline-capable adaptive learning tools that prioritize inclusivity and cultural context. This work aligns directly with Sustainable Development Goal 4: Quality Education, which calls for equitable access to learning opportunities for all.

Ultimately, GLASS reshaped how I view engineering, not just as a technical discipline, but as a tool for global impact. It strengthened my commitment to building human-centered AI systems and continues to influence my goal of developing scalable, accessible technologies that expand educational opportunity worldwide.

sdgs.name: Quality Education
sdgs.description: Ensure inclusive and equitable quality education and promote lifelong learning opportunities for all.

sdgs.name: Climate Action
sdgs.description: Take urgent action to combat climate change and its impacts.

journey.title: Admitted to GLASS Honors Program
journey.description: Selected among 25 scholars at NYU Tandon recognized for academic distinction and global leadership potential. Began a 3-year interdisciplinary journey connecting engineering to the world's most critical challenges.

journey.title: ADRA Beach Cleanup
journey.description: Volunteered with ADRA during J-term 2024 for an environmental cleanup initiative on Rio's beaches. Brought an engineering perspective to understand waste management challenges and coastal ecosystem preservation in a developing urban context.
journey.imageAlt: ADRA beach cleanup volunteer in Rio de Janeiro

journey.title: NYU Abu Dhabi Study Abroad
journey.description: Completed a 4-month study abroad at NYU Abu Dhabi. Immersed in an international academic environment with students from 80+ countries. Built Health Reflect — an intelligent smart mirror monitoring indoor air quality using real-time environmental sensors and a Raspberry Pi.
journey.imageAlt: Chris and friends in the Abu Dhabi desert

journey.title: VinUniversity — Agent-Based Urban Modeling
journey.description: Participated in a project-based engineering program at VinUniversity (Cornell/UPenn collaboration). Collaborated with Vietnamese and international students to design urban road systems using agent-based modeling. Studied sustainable urban planning under Professor Nguyen Ngoc Doanh. Rapid Ride won first place for best pitch and program at VinUniversity's competition.

journey.title: Whirlpool ML Internship
journey.description: Secured through networking at SHPE National Conference. Joined the Machine Learning team to develop computer vision algorithms detecting camera obstructions in KitchenAid smart ovens, achieving 94% detection accuracy. Presented findings directly to KitchenAid stakeholders at an executive review.

journey.title: SHPE National Conference
journey.description: Attended the Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers National Conference. Connected with engineers across industries including aerospace, tech, and consumer electronics. Secured the Whirlpool ML internship through relationships built at the event — a direct example of the professional development GLASS cultivates.

journey.title: NYU Climate Week Kickoff
journey.description: Served as volunteer coordinator for NYU's Climate Week Kickoff, organizing a sustainable art installation that engaged the campus community in environmental storytelling. Helped connect STEM students with the broader climate action narrative.

journey.title: NYU × Amazon Mini Hackathon
journey.description: Built an Amazon Alexa skill for automated coding interview moderation as part of the NYU × Amazon hackathon. The skill guided candidates through structured technical questions with real-time feedback, demonstrating the intersection of voice AI and software engineering education.

journey.title: GLASS Capstone — Paper & Poster
journey.description: The culminating GLASS project: a research paper and public poster presentation synthesizing the full three-year journey. Will connect engineering work across Abu Dhabi, Vietnam, Rio de Janeiro, and New York to at least two UN SDGs, demonstrating globally competent and socially responsible engineering practice.
